headline: Georgia and Washington seek redemption in must-win clash
subhead: Two ranked teams at 3-4 face elimination scenarios in Week 8.

xFactor.why: Georgia's secondary must contain Washington's passing game after Texas exposed serious coverage breakdowns.

keyMatchup: Georgia's run game sustainability against a Washington defense that has allowed 47 points; controlling time of possession becomes the deciding factor.
body: Both programs arrive in Seattle treading water at 3-4, with bowl eligibility slipping away by the week. Georgia's one-point loss to Penn State showed competitive mettle, but the staff cannot afford another close defeat when the margin for error has vanished. Washington, meanwhile, suffered a 26-point humbling at Texas that raises urgent questions about execution and defensive fundamentals.

The visitor holds a slight edge in recent quality of loss, and Georgia's ability to establish a physical ground game will be critical against a Washington unit that has been gashed repeatedly. If Georgia's offense can eat clock and limit possessions, Washington's inconsistent passing attack will struggle to keep pace. The loser here faces genuine desperation in the back half of the schedule.
